review_execution.cost_metadata_unavailable_reason: Claude CLI text output did not expose cost metadata for this non-persistent review run; no second review was executed solely to fetch metadata.

findings.title: production_ready asserted while RP00 contract validator is red and evidence pack is absent
findings.detail: current_subphase.status and closeout_row.status are both 'production_ready', minimum_closeout_status='production_ready', and production_ready_flag_present=true, with evidence_model='closeout_pack', closeout_pack_ref='CP00-029', evidence_root='docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029'. But known_pre_evidence_gate states `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` currently FAILS because the cp00-029 pack evidence files are not yet created, and git_diff_stat contains no docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029 entries. The closeout status therefore asserts a completion that the declared evidence model cannot substantiate; the green unit suite (483/0) is a separate surface from the red RP00 contract validator.
findings.evidence: current_subphase.status=production_ready; closeout_row.status=production_ready; production_ready_flag_present=true; evidence_root=docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029; known_pre_evidence_gate (validator fails: cp00-029 evidence files not created); git_diff_stat has no docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029 files.
findings.recommendation: Author the cp00-029 evidence files under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029 and re-run `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` to green BEFORE treating S01 as closed or handing off to S02 as complete. Do not propagate production_ready while the gate is red.

findings.title: Upstream dependency on RP00.P02.M06.S11 is declared but not evidenced in-pack
findings.detail: The forward chain is consistent (closeout_state_policy.next_subphase and fixture_summary.next_subphase both = RP00.P02.M07.S02; fixture future_subphases lists S02–S20). The backward/cross-micro-phase link rests solely on no_write_scope.depends_on_RP00_P02_M06_S11=true; the packet carries no completion status for M06.S11 and no confirmation that the context IDs/contract S01 consumes match M06.S11's published outputs.
findings.evidence: no_write_scope.depends_on_RP00_P02_M06_S11=true; closeout_state_policy.next_subphase=RP00.P02.M07.S02; fixture_summary.next_subphase=RP00.P02.M07.S02; no M06.S11 status present in packet.
findings.recommendation: Confirm RP00.P02.M06.S11 is itself at/above its minimum closeout status and that S01's imported CONTROL_PLANE_TEST_AND_GOLDEN_CASE_SET_CONTEXT_IDS / contract align with M06.S11's outputs before finalizing the handoff.

findings.area: validator / test coverage and no-write boundary
findings.title: No-write behavior and assertion depth not verifiable from presence flags alone
findings.detail: test/service.test.js is 141 inserted lines yet `node --test` reports 483 pass / 0 fail, implying heavily parameterized/data-driven subtests. code_presence_checks tests_no_write=true, tests_bind_s01=true, tests_fixture=true are presence indicators, not behavioral proof. As a read-only reviewer I cannot execute to confirm the 483 assertions exercise real contract semantics and actually observe the absence of DB/storage/state mutation (writes_product_state=false, does_not_write_database/storage/persist_product_state, does_not_execute_service_logic) rather than merely asserting shape/exports.
findings.evidence: git_diff_stat test/service.test.js=141 insertions; tests_already_run=483 pass/0 fail; code_presence_checks.tests_no_write=true, tests_fixture=true; no_write_scope writes_product_state=false and does_not_* flags.
findings.recommendation: Verify the suite asserts the no-write boundary by observing no mutation/persistence and no service-logic execution (negative assertions), and that parameterized cases check meaningful contract behavior, not just field presence.

findings.title: S01 is 1 of 20 in M07 — correctly non-completing; guard against future rollup drift
findings.detail: This pack correctly avoids premature completion: source_micro_phase_completed=false and does_not_complete_RP00_P02_M07, does_not_complete_RP00_P02, does_not_close_RP00, does_not_replace_H00_or_C00 are all true. fixture_summary.future_subphases enumerates S02–S20, so M07 has 19 subphases remaining. No defect now; this is a watch item for downstream packs.
findings.recommendation: Keep source_micro_phase_completed=false until S20 closes and ensure micro-phase/phase/RP00 rollups remain gated on all child subphases in subsequent packs.

security_notes: Claude found the no-write metadata-only boundary and no premature M07/P02/RP00 completion claims internally consistent in the supplied packet.
Claude P1 was evidence-gate sequencing, not runtime security behavior; it is resolved by creating CP00-029 evidence and passing final gates.
test_notes: CP00-029 records one valid pack-level Claude Opus 4.8 max read-only review; earlier zero-output attempts are not counted.
raw_review.summary: CP00-029 implements the RP00.P02.M07.S01 'Test And Golden Case Set service entrypoint contract' as a metadata/contract-only change across the 8 declared target files, with the no-write boundary, required exports, and forward/backward ledger links all internally consistent within the packet. The unit suite is green (483 pass / 0 fail) and all code-presence checks are true. However, the pack cannot be approved for closeout as-is: the subphase is already flagged status=production_ready (production_ready_flag_present=true) while its declared evidence model is unsatisfied — the cp00-029 evidence files under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-029 do not exist (absent from git_diff_stat) and the authoritative gate `npm run rp00:control-plane:validate` is RED per known_pre_evidence_gate. A production_ready closeout whose own contract validator fails is internally inconsistent. The implementation correctly avoids premature M07/P02/RP00 completion (source_micro_phase_completed=false; does_not_complete_* all true) and stays in-lane (does_not_execute_service_logic=true; service.js is a thin 35-line contract module, no scope creep into S02 request_normalization). Resolve the evidence gate, then this is approvable.

items: Primary blocker: cp00-029 evidence files do not exist and the RP00 contract validator is red; closeout cannot be finalized and production_ready is currently unsubstantiated by its own evidence model (F1).
production_ready is already set in the ledger ahead of a green gate, creating risk that downstream consumers trust S01 as closed while the validator fails (F1).
Cross-micro-phase handoff from M06.S11 is asserted, not verified within this pack; contract drift between M06.S11 outputs and S01 inputs cannot be ruled out from the packet (F2).

No-write boundary and 483-assertion suite are validated only by presence flags and a pass count; behavioral enforcement (no writes, no service-logic execution) is not independently confirmable in this read-only review (F3).
M07 is only 1/20 complete; integration, persistence-boundary, and approval-routing risks are correctly deferred to later subphases (S02–S20) and remain open by design (F4).

adjudication_notes: Raw Claude findings F1 P1, F2 P2, F3 P2, and F4 P3 were adjudicated. F1 is resolved by creating the required CP00-029 evidence files and final RP00 validation. F2 is resolved by CP00-028 prerequisite evidence and RP00 validator requiredClosedSubphases/requiredEvidenceRefs coverage for RP00.P02.M06.S11. F3 is resolved by direct no-write assertions, frozen metadata contracts, fixture mirror checks, and absence of DB/storage/product-state adapters in the S01 module. F4 is a downstream watch item and correctly deferred because M07 remains open through S20.
